title: Power tool with electric motor and auxiliary switch path
abstract: A power tool is configured to receive power from a power supply. The power tool may include a rectifier that may output a rectified signal to a DC power bus. A switching arrangement may operate to deliver electric power from the DC power bus to an electric motor. A switch path may be electrically coupled in parallel with the rectifier on the DC power bus. The switch path includes an auxiliary capacitor in series with a switch and a state of the switch controls a discharging path for the auxiliary capacitor. A switch control circuit may be configured to detect voltage associated with at least one of the AC power supply or the DC power bus and to control state of the switch in accordance with magnitude of the detected voltage.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . A power tool configured to receive power from a power supply, the power tool comprising: an electric motor; a rectifier configured to receive an alternating current from an alternating current (AC) power supply and output a rectified signal supplied to a DC power bus; a switching arrangement having a plurality of motor switches connected electrically between the DC power bus and the electric motor, and operates to deliver electric power from the DC power bus to the electric motor; a switch path electrically coupled in parallel with the rectifier on the DC power bus, wherein the switch path includes an auxiliary capacitor in series with a switch and state of the switch controls a discharging path for the auxiliary capacitor; and a switch control circuit configured to detect voltage associated with at least one of the AC power supply or the DC power bus and to control state of the switch in accordance with magnitude of the detected voltage. 2. The power tool of claim 1 wherein the switch control circuit includes a sensing circuit and a comparator, the sensing circuit is configured to detect the voltage across the DC power bus, and the comparator is configured to compare the voltage detected with a predetermined threshold and outputs a signal to the switch to control the state of the switch. 3. The power tool of claim 2 wherein: the sensing circuit includes a voltage divider that is electrically coupled to the DC power bus and the comparator, and the comparator includes a transistor and a diode, the diode is coupled between the DC power bus and the emitter of the transistor, the base of the transistor is coupled to the voltage divider, and the collector of the transistor is coupled to the switch to control the state of the switch. 4. The power tool of claim 1 further comprising: a DC bus capacitor electrically coupled in parallel with the rectifier and electrically connected between the rectifier and the switching arrangement, wherein the capacitance of the DC bus capacitor is less than capacitance of the auxiliary capacitor. 5. The power tool of claim 4 wherein the DC bus capacitor has a capacitance of about 5 F to 20pF. 6. The power tool of claim 1 wherein the switch control circuit opens the switch when the detected voltage is less than or equal to a first voltage threshold to prevent current flow through the discharge path and closes the switch to allow current flow through the discharge path when the detected voltage is greater than a second voltage threshold greater than the first voltage threshold. 7. The power tool of claim 1 wherein the electric motor is a brushless motor. 8. The power tool of claim 1 wherein the switch control circuit is coupled to at least one node of the DC power bus. 9. The power tool of claim 1 wherein the switch control circuit includes a sensing circuit and a comparator, the sensing circuit detects voltage across the auxiliary capacitor, and the comparator is configured to compare the voltage detected with a predetermined threshold and outputs a signal to the switch to control the state of the switch. 10. The power tool of claim 1 further comprising: a first housing that houses the electric motor and the switching arrangement; and a second housing separate from the first housing that houses the switch path and the switch control circuit, wherein the second housing is configured to 

 connect to the first housing to electrically couple the switch path in parallel with the DC power bus. 1 1 . A power tool configured to receive power from a power supply, the power tool comprising: an electric motor; a power bus including positive and negative lines; a switching arrangement having a plurality of motor switches and operable to deliver electric power from the power bus to the electric motor; a DC bus capacitor electrically coupled across the power bus; an auxiliary switch path electrically coupled in parallel with the DC bus capacitor, wherein the auxiliary switch path includes an auxiliary capacitor in series with a switch, the capacitance of the DC bus capacitor is less than capacitance of the auxiliary capacitor, the switch forms a first current passageway through the auxiliary capacitor to charge the auxiliary capacitor when in a first state and forms a second current passageway through the auxiliary capacitor to discharge the auxiliary capacitor when in a second state; and a switch control circuit configured to detect voltage of the power bus and to control state of the switch in accordance with magnitude of the detected voltage. 12. The power tool of claim 1 1 wherein: the switch includes a transistor, and the switch control circuit is configured to activate and deactivate the transistor based on the detected voltage. 13. The power tool of claim 12 wherein: in the first state, the transistor electrically decouples the auxiliary capacitor from the power bus to have current flow through the auxiliary capacitor via the first current passageway, and in the second state, the transistor electrically couples the auxiliary capacitor to the power bus to have current flow through the auxiliary capacitor via the first current passageway and the second current passageway. 14. The power tool of claim 1 1 wherein the switch control circuit includes a sensing circuit and a comparator, the sensing circuit is configured to detect the voltage across at least one of the power bus and the auxiliary capacitor, and the comparator compares the voltage detected with a predetermined threshold and outputs a signal to the switch to control the state of the switch. 15. The power tool of claim 1 1 wherein the switch control circuit controls the switch in the first state when the detected voltage is less than or equal to a first voltage threshold and controls the switch in the second state when the detected voltage is greater than a second voltage threshold greater than the first voltage threshold. 16. The power tool of claim 1 1 wherein the DC bus capacitor has a capacitance of about 10 F to 20 F. 17. The power tool of claim 1 1 wherein the electric motor is a brushless motor. 18. The power tool of claim 1 1 further comprising: a housing that houses the electric motor, the switching arrangement, and the DC bus capacitor; a power supply interface positioned along a surface of the housing; and an AC power adapter configured to attach to the power supply interface and that includes an auxiliary capacitor circuit, wherein the auxiliary capacitor circuit includes the auxiliary switch path and the switch control circuit, the auxiliary switch path is electrically coupled to the power bus when the AC power adapter is attached to the power supply interface and is electrically decoupled from the power bus when the AC power adapter is detached from the power supply interface. 19. A power tool configured to receive power from a power supply, the power tool comprising: a brushless electric motor; a rectifier configured to receive an alternating current from an alternating current (AC) power supply and operable to convert the alternating current to a rectified current supplied to a DC bus; a switching arrangement having a plurality of motor switches connected electrically between the rectifier and the brushless the electric motor, and operates to deliver the rectified current from the rectifier to the electric motor; an auxiliary switch path electrically coupled across the DC bus in parallel with the rectifier, wherein the auxiliary switch path includes an auxiliary capacitor in series with a transistor; a DC bus capacitor electrically coupled in parallel with the rectifier, wherein the capacitance of the DC bus capacitor is less than capacitance of the auxiliary capacitor; and a switch control circuit configured to detect voltage associated with the power supply or the DC bus and to control state of auxiliary capacitor by way the transistor in accordance with magnitude of the detected voltage, wherein the switch control circuit controls the transistor in an OFF state to charge the auxiliary capacitor when the detected voltage is less than or equal to a first voltage threshold and controls the transistor in an ON state to discharge the auxiliary capacitor when the detected voltage is greater than a second voltage threshold greater than the first voltage threshold. 20. The power tool of claim 19 wherein the transistor is an IGBT with an anti-parallel diode. 21 . The power tool of claim 19 wherein the auxiliary capacitor discharges to the DC bus when the transistor is in the ON state. 22. A power adapter for use with a power tool and configured to supply electric power from an alternating current power supply to the power tool, wherein the power tool includes an electric motor, the power adapter comprising: 

 a housing; and an auxiliary capacitor circuit housed within the housing and including an auxiliary capacitor, a switch in series with the auxiliary capacitor, and a switch control circuit, wherein a state of the switch creates either a charging path for the auxiliary capacitor or both the charging path and a discharging path for the auxiliary capacitor, and the switch control circuit is configured to detect voltage associated with the power supply or the power tool and to control state of the switch in accordance with magnitude of the detected voltage. 23. The power adapter of claim 22 wherein the switch control circuit includes a sensing circuit and a comparator, the sensing circuit is configured to detect the voltage across at least one of a DC bus provided in the power tool and the auxiliary capacitor, and the comparator compares the voltage detected with a predetermined threshold and outputs a signal to the switch to control the state of the switch. 24. The power adapter of claim 23 wherein: the sensing circuit includes a voltage divider that is electrically coupled to the comparator and is configured to electrically couple to the DC bus, and the comparator includes a transistor and a diode, the diode is configured to electrical couple between the DC bus and the emitter of the transistor, the base of the transistor is coupled to the voltage divider, and the collector of the transistor is coupled to the switch to control the state of the switch. 25. The power adapter of claim 22 further comprising: one or more terminals electrically coupled to the auxiliary capacitor circuit and configured to connect to an interface provided on the power tool, wherein with the terminals connected to the interface of the power tool, the auxiliary capacitor and the switch form an auxiliary switch path that is electrically coupled across a DC bus of the power tool, and the switch control circuit is electrically coupled to the DC bus for detecting the voltage along the DC bus. 26. The power adapter of claim 22 further comprising: a rectifier configured to receive an alternating current from the alternating current power supply and operable to convert the alternating current to a rectified current supplied to a DC bus; and a DC bus capacitor electrically coupled in parallel with the rectifier, wherein the capacitance of the DC bus capacitor is less than capacitance of the auxiliary capacitor.
